metadata.author: John
metadata.subject: The Revelation of Jesus Christ as the Center of God’s Economy according to God’s Administration — the Consummation of the Divine Revelation.

1: The revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave to Him to show to His slaves...
I was in spirit on the Lord's Day, and I heard behind me a loud voice like a trumpet,
Saying, What you see write in a scroll and send it to the seven churches: to Ephesus and to Smyrna and to Pergamos and to Thyatira and to Sardis and to Philadelphia and to Laodicea.
21: And I saw a new heaven and a new earth; for the first heaven and the first earth passed away, and the sea is no more.
And I saw the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.
22: And he showed me a river of water of life, bright as crystal, proceeding out of the throne of God and of the Lamb in the middle of its street.
And on this side and on that side of the river was the tree of life, producing twelve fruits, yielding its fruit each month; and the leaves of the tree were for the healing of the nations.
The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all the saints. Amen.

10.text: To see the visions of this book, one must be in their human spirit. It is in the spirit that we receive the revelation of the Lord's move and the church's destiny.

2.text: The New Jerusalem is not a physical city but the corporate expression of the Triune God with His redeemed, regenerated, and transformed people for eternity.

21.text: The Bible begins with God's creation and ends with grace. This grace is the processed Triune God meeting all our needs for the fulfillment of His eternal purpose.
